growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom's fiscal Q2 release showed $10.8 billion of AI semiconductor revenue, and the stock has also been lifted by Apple's reported $30 billion multiyear chip partnership extension, which keeps Broadcom squarely in the custom-silicon and AI-networking winners' circle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom has one of the cleanest AI monetization paths through custom accelerators, networking, and large free cash flow.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The stock has been hit by AI capex worries and is still around 20% below its highs, but the business remains best-in-class and the July 29 earnings date gives a clear inflection point if Azure and AI monetization stay solid.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft is the highest-quality AI/cloud compounder in the batch and has a near-term earnings catalyst after a meaningful drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TrendForce said on July 3 that AI server demand should keep DRAM and NAND contract prices rising in 3Q26, while recent market coverage shows Micron shares rallying on the expectation that AI memory demand and HBM tightness will keep the cycle firm.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron screens as the strongest cyclical value setup because AI-driven memory tightness supports earnings while the forward multiple remains low.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TSMC shows persistent insider buying across multiple VP-level purchases, even as the market worries about AI spending; the long-term AI supply-chain position remains one of the cleanest in semis.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TSMC is the core AI foundry winner with raised growth expectations, but geopolitical and capex risks argue for buy rather than strong buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lockheed's July 23 earnings surge and June 24 THAAD award, which can reach $35 billion and quadruple interceptor production, make it a direct beneficiary of the Iran/Ukraine air-defense rearmament cycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed's Q2 beat, raised guidance, record backlog, and THAAD ramp make it a clean defense-cycle beneficiary.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TE Connectivity reported July 22 results with record $5.7 billion orders and explicitly tied growth to AI and data-center interconnect demand, while its data-center business was already running at an $800 million revenue pace in the latest quarter commentary.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TE Connectivity is a reasonably valued AI-infrastructure and electrification compounder after record orders and above-guidance earnings.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Salesforce is still roughly 37% below its 52-week high even after a bounce, but the market appears to have reset expectations more than broken the franchise, and analyst sentiment remains constructive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce has reset to a value multiple while Agentforce ARR and free cash flow show the AI transition is starting to monetize.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Intuit is still being treated by the market as a possible AI disruption target because tax prep and small-business workflows are prime agentic-AI targets, even though the company is pushing its own AI agents and Anthropic partnership as a defense.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Intuit is a high-margin, cash-rich franchise trading at a reset multiple while actively defending tax and small-business workflows with AI agents.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Chevron is riding the same energy rotation as Exxon, with oil-price support and still meaningful distance from its highs, so it remains a straightforward way to express the sector bid.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy rotation plus Iraq/Hormuz-bypass optionality supports CVX, but upside remains commodity-dependent rather than deeply mispriced.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Alphabet delivered a strong earnings print, but the stock sold off after management raised 2026 capex guidance sharply, creating a fresh catalyst-driven reset rather than a core business problem.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Alphabet's core ad and cloud growth justify buying the capex-driven reset, but free-cash-flow pressure caps conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv said on July 21 it is expanding manufacturing and testing capacity for AI-ready data center cooling, with the new Italy campus investment aimed at doubling regional chiller output by the end of 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv remains a direct AI power and cooling winner, but the stock already prices in a lot of execution.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow reported today that ServiceNow AI crossed $1 billion in annual contract value in Q2 2026, which is the clearest proof in this set of real AI monetization rather than feature marketing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow's Q2 showed real AI monetization and durable enterprise demand, but valuation and software sentiment keep this below strong buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: FIS is roughly 51% below its 52-week high and only a little above its low, yet it trades at a single-digit forward multiple and has a dated Aug. 4 Q2 earnings catalyst plus a July 16 Anthropic partnership update; the bear case is that the Worldpay hangover and leverage still keep the multiple capped if execution slips.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FIS is a cheap post-drawdown fintech with an earnings catalyst and Anthropic AI optionality, offset by leverage risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The CEO bought shares in late June, and the stock has continued to outperform peers while still sitting below its highs, which lines up with the broader energy strength and insider confidence signal.
growth_picks.analyst_take: OXY has insider-confirmed energy upside and improving debt optics, but limited target upside and commodity exposure keep conviction moderate.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: T-Mobile beat earnings, raised free-cash-flow guidance, and kept subscriber momentum intact, yet the stock sold off on a small revenue miss, which often creates a good post-earnings entry point in a cash-flow compounder.
growth_picks.analyst_take: T-Mobile's post-earnings selloff looks overdone given EPS beat, subscriber adds, and raised free-cash-flow guidance.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX and NATO agreed on July 7 to expand AMRAAM production in Europe, and RTX later raised guidance as missile and air-defense demand kept climbing with the Ukraine and Middle East conflicts.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has a strong defense and commercial-aerospace backlog catalyst, but valuation and proximity to highs cap upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's July 7 purchase of up to five MQ-4C Triton drones gives Northrop a direct order stream for maritime ISR as alliance tension pushes spending toward surveillance and deterrence assets.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop's record backlog and NATO ISR catalyst support a buy, but margin pressure keeps conviction below high.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's export-control pressure on MP and other U.S. rare-earth firms keeps the non-China magnet supply chain strategically scarce, supporting MP's role after fresh U.S. government backing and Apple/GM offtake momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is a strategic U.S. rare-earth supply-chain winner with policy and customer support, but cash burn makes it speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: ConocoPhillips agreed on July 17 to buy a 42% interest in BP's Kirkuk redevelopment, giving it direct exposure to a large Iraqi oilfield expansion tied to the region's push for routes less vulnerable to Hormuz disruption.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ConocoPhillips adds a disciplined Iraq redevelopment option to a cash-generative E&P, but commodity and geopolitical risk remain central.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: President/CEO Ross R. Bhappu bought 74,000 shares on July 7 at $13.08 for about $967,920, a large open-market commitment that stands out because it came from the new CEO and pushed his holdings up 41%.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy Fuels has insider-confirmed asymmetry in uranium and critical materials, but losses and financing risk demand small sizing.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Berkshire Hathaway's Q1 2026 holdings list a 39,809,456-share Delta position valued at about $2.65 billion and marked as a Buy, which is a meaningful large-cap institutional stamp of approval.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Delta combines low valuation, positive FCF, strong premium demand, and a Berkshire 13F signal, but fuel risk caps conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Applied Digital has a 27.38% short float with 70.99 million shares short, but fiscal Q3 2026 revenue jumped 139% to $126.6 million and adjusted EBITDA reached $44.1 million, which gives the short base a real fundamental squeeze risk.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Applied Digital has a volatile but credible AI data-center squeeze setup with triple-digit revenue growth and a near-term earnings catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: SailPoint is still heavily shorted at 29.94% of float, yet FY2026 revenue rose 23% to $295 million, ARR increased 28% to $1.125 billion, and management raised FY2027 guidance, so the setup has both growth and cover potential.
growth_picks.analyst_take: SailPoint combines identity-security growth, rising ARR and high short interest, while low balance-sheet debt reduces the usual small-cap software risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Adobe reported record Q2 results on June 11 and then expanded its Creative Agent across Firefly and Creative Cloud in June, giving it a credible enterprise and creator-side AI monetization path rather than a standalone model story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe is cheap for a high-margin software franchise and Q2 proved AI monetization is real, but execution risk from AI disruption keeps conviction capped.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: O-I Glass is down about 47% from its 52-week high and still near its low, but it has a July 28 earnings release, a July 29 call, and screens at a cheap forward multiple with next-year EPS back in positive territory; the bear case is that the company is still losing money today and leverage is high enough that any margin miss can hurt again.
growth_picks.analyst_take: O-I Glass is a leveraged turnaround with a cheap forward multiple and near-term earnings catalyst, but it needs proof of margin stabilization.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: With Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb disruption driving LPG shipping rates higher, Dorian LPG's VLGC fleet is a direct beneficiary of the war-risk premium and rerouting of Middle East cargoes.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Dorian LPG is a direct beneficiary of elevated VLGC rates tied to Middle East shipping disruption, with earnings leverage still visible.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Capricor has an FDA review window tied to deramiocel plus a fresh clinical-data backdrop, making it one of the cleaner biotech catalyst names on the calendar over the next few weeks.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Capricor has the cleanest near-term biotech catalyst in the batch, backed by HOPE-3 data and a dated FDA review, but it remains binary.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista launched a next-generation 1.6T portfolio for AI fabrics on June 9 and followed it with a July 21 AI-driven zero trust branch release, reinforcing that its networking stack is being pulled deeper into AI cluster and enterprise edge deployments.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Arista is a high-quality AI networking compounder, but valuation and proximity to highs leave limited margin of safety.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: SiteOne is about 39% below its 52-week high and only a few percent above its low, yet it remains profitable and has a July 28 earnings release and July 29 call that can reset the housing and landscaping narrative; the bear case is that the stock still trades on a premium multiple, so a mild miss can compress it further.
growth_picks.analyst_take: SiteOne has a deep-drawdown setup and near-term earnings catalyst, but stagnant growth and premium valuation make the risk/reward unproven.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Hims has a 29.29% short float with 61.17 million shares short, while Q1 2026 revenue was about $608 million, up 4% year over year, subscribers grew 9%, and management raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to $2.8 billion-$3.0 billion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Short squeeze potential is real, but GLP-1 transition risk, negative earnings, analyst downside and recent insider selling keep it out of buy territory.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Dollar Tree authorized a new $2.5 billion share repurchase program on July 2 and said it had roughly $700 million left under the prior authorization after repurchasing $500 million in June, which is aggressive capital return support at current prices.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Dollar Tree has defensive earnings and a large buyback, but the stock is near target price and no longer offers enough upside to chase.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: IBM is down about 39% from its 52-week high and just below its 52-week low after the July 22 Q2 warning and revenue-guidance cut, but it is still profitable, keeps strong free cash flow, and the selloff looks like a capitulation reset rather than a broken franchise; the bear case is that delayed enterprise deals and AI-related spending shifts could take longer to repair than the market wants.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IBM's free cash flow and valuation argue against panic selling, but the Q2 warning exposed real execution and AI-budget displacement risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lululemon is nearly 50% below its 52-week high and only about 8% above the low after June guidance cuts and a fresh July 16 downgrade, with the next earnings window now pushed into late August or early September; the bear case is that the slowdown may be structural if demand and margin recovery do not re-accelerate.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lululemon is statistically cheap and cash-generative, but guidance cuts make it too early to call a durable turn.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Energy remains the strongest major sector in July, and Exxon still offers exposure to a favorable oil tape at a valuation that is cheaper than the market despite the recent run.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Exxon is a high-quality energy exposure with an imminent earnings catalyst, but current upside is not compelling after the sector rotation move.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Royal Caribbean still trades well below its peak even after strong demand, so the setup is a classic post-run contrarian one where good fundamentals are not fully reflected in the stock price.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Royal Caribbean demand remains strong, but leverage and limited margin of safety keep it from a fresh buy at current price.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Senior EVP/COO Steven M. Klein bought 15,000 shares on July 20 at $10.00 for about $150,000, which is a clean open-market insider buy in a bank that is already in the middle of a merger/conversion catalyst.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Columbia Financial has a real conversion and merger catalyst, but post-transaction dilution and weak analyst upside make the setup too specialized for a broad buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Duolingo remains a watchlist avoid candidate because AI tutors can commoditize basic language practice, and recent coverage still frames the stock around AI disruption risk even as the company uses AI to scale content production.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Duolingo has strong product metrics and cash generation after a massive drawdown, but AI-tutor disruption and limited target upside block a buy call.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: A late-August FDA decision on ONS-5010 creates a classic binary biotech setup, and the calendar model still assigns a meaningful approval probability despite prior regulatory friction.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Outlook has a near-term FDA binary event, but prior CRLs and weak liquidity make it too risky to underwrite as a buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Short interest is elevated and the stock has been trying to reclaim trend levels, which creates a contrarian setup if hotel demand and franchise execution keep stabilizing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Choice Hotels has short-squeeze ingredients and an upcoming report, but leverage, slow growth and limited target upside argue for patience.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Cloudflare is trying to turn AI crawling and agentic usage into a paid transaction layer through pay-per-use and Monetization Gateway experiments, creating a plausible new revenue stream if publishers and AI platforms adopt it.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cloudflare's AI-content monetization idea is strategically interesting, but the valuation is already extreme and leaves no room for execution risk.
